_comment: ac7 team config. Defines one team with a directive, roles, and slots. Slot tokens are SHA-256 hashed on disk (tokenHash); adding a plaintext `token` field auto-hashes it on next boot and rewrites the file. Each slot has an `authority` tier — one of `director | manager | individual-contributor`, defaulting to `individual-contributor` when omitted. At least one director is required. Directors can do anything; managers can create/assign objectives but not edit team config; individual-contributors participate in comms and execute objectives assigned to them. Slots may also carry a `totpSecret` (base32) for web-UI login — generated by the first-run wizard or by `ac7 enroll`. The optional `https` block controls the HTTPS listener (omit for localhost HTTP). VAPID keys for Web Push are auto-generated on first boot and persisted into a top-level `webPush` block not shown here.

team.name: my-team
team.directive: Ship and operate the payment processing service.
team.context: We own the full lifecycle of the payment service: code, CI, deploys, and incident response. Our operating window is 0900-1700 UTC.

individual-contributor.description: Directs the team, makes go/no-go calls, handles escalations.
individual-contributor.instructions: The individual-contributor role in this team directs activity in the team channel and handles escalations. Issue clear directives and keep the team aligned on the directive.

implementer.instructions: The implementer role in this team takes direction from command, ships changes, and reports progress in the team channel.

reviewer.instructions: The reviewer role in this team verifies implementer output before it ships and posts approve/request-changes decisions with rationale.

watcher.description: Monitors team activity and flags anomalies.
watcher.instructions: The watcher role in this team passively observes and surfaces unusual signals to command.
